title: aion-worker (Rust) heartbeat and cooperative cancellation delivery
purpose: Connect the ActivityContext surfaces to the live session: a handler's ctx.heartbeat() reaches the engine, and an engine-issued cancellation flips the context's flag so a cooperative handler can stop. This is what keeps a long-running activity alive in the engine's eyes and lets it be cancelled without forced termination.
task: Implement protocol/heartbeat.rs (heartbeat frame send + heartbeat-timeout bookkeeping) and wire the ActivityContext heartbeat seam and cancellation-flag setter from AR-003 to the WorkerSession. Heartbeats carry an optional opaque Payload of progress detail. Cancellation is delivered through the session and flips the context flag; the SDK never kills the handler task. Do not implement reconnect (AR-005).

requirements.title: Heartbeat frame send
requirements.spec: WHEN a handler calls ctx.heartbeat(detail), THE SYSTEM SHALL send a heartbeat frame for the activity through the session carrying the optional opaque Payload detail. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT heartbeat on the handler's behalf.
acceptance: protocol/heartbeat.rs implements sending a heartbeat for an ActivityId via WorkerSession::send_heartbeat, carrying detail: Option<Payload>.
ctx.heartbeat() (the seam from AR-003) now routes to this send; the detail Payload's content-type tag is preserved per CO7.
No background timer or thread emits heartbeats automatically — only an explicit ctx.heartbeat() call sends one (per D4).
A test with the fake session asserts that a handler calling ctx.heartbeat(Some(detail)) twice results in two heartbeat frames for the correct ActivityId carrying the detail.

requirements.title: Heartbeat-timeout bookkeeping surface
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL track the last-heartbeat instant per in-flight activity so the worker can surface a locally-observable liveness view. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT itself fail an activity for a missed heartbeat (the engine owns heartbeat-timeout enforcement).
acceptance: heartbeat.rs records the last-heartbeat time per ActivityId, updated on each send.

The bookkeeping is exposed for observability/logging only; the SDK does not terminate or fail a handler on a missed local heartbeat — the engine enforces the heartbeat timeout (documented, per the boundary with AW).
A unit test asserts the last-heartbeat timestamp advances on each ctx.heartbeat() call.

requirements.title: Cooperative cancellation delivery
requirements.spec: WHEN the engine signals cancellation for an in-flight activity through the session, THE SYSTEM SHALL flip that activity's ActivityContext cancellation flag. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT forcibly terminate the handler's task.
acceptance: A cancellation signal received on the session (per the aion-proto cancel frame) sets the cancellation handle for the matching ActivityId, so ctx.is_cancelled() returns true and ctx.cancelled() resolves.
The handler task is not aborted, dropped, or killed by the SDK; it runs until it returns (cooperative only, per D5 / CO9).
If a handler ignores cancellation and returns Ok, the result is still reported; the engine discards it (documented — the SDK does not suppress the report).
A test signals cancellation mid-execution and asserts the handler observes ctx.is_cancelled() == true and is allowed to return on its own; assert the SDK issued no abort.

boundaries: SHALL NOT enforce heartbeat timeouts or activity timeouts — the engine owns that (AW/AT).
SHALL NOT forcibly kill, abort, or drop a running handler task — cooperative cancellation only (CO9).
verification: cargo test -p aion-worker passes, including heartbeat and cooperative-cancellation tests.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
grep confirms no task abort/kill of a running handler (no JoinHandle::abort on the handler task).
